title: Protect ISRO Maneuver Brief

briefing.headline: OrbitGuard recommends a low-delta-v avoidance maneuver for Protect ISRO.
briefing.summary: Recommend candidate mnv-protect-isro-a: 0.12 m/s along track prograde burn 4.0 hours before TCA. Estimated Pc changes from 2.779e-04 to 3.973e-173, while miss distance changes from 611.8 m to 8.39 km. Secondary screening status is clear: No new critical conjunctions introduced by the selected Protect ISRO maneuver. Screened objects: 3.
key_points: Recommend candidate mnv-protect-isro-a: 0.12 m/s along track prograde burn 4.0 hours before TCA.
Estimated Pc changes from 2.779e-04 to 3.973e-173, while miss distance changes from 611.8 m to 8.39 km.
Secondary screening status is clear: No new critical conjunctions introduced by the selected Protect ISRO maneuver. Screened objects: 3.
limitations: Pc uses an assumed covariance model because public TLEs do not include covariance.
Maneuver effects use the documented Round 1 along-track lead-time surrogate.
This is a simulation recommendation, not spacecraft command authority.

sections.title: Decision
sections.body: Recommend candidate mnv-protect-isro-a: 0.12 m/s along track prograde burn 4.0 hours before TCA.

sections.title: Risk Reduction
sections.body: Estimated Pc changes from 2.779e-04 to 3.973e-173, while miss distance changes from 611.8 m to 8.39 km.

sections.title: Secondary Screening
sections.body: Secondary screening status is clear: No new critical conjunctions introduced by the selected Protect ISRO maneuver. Screened objects: 3.

sections.title: Audit Trail
sections.body: Scenario run scenario-run-protect-isro-001; conjunction conj-protect-isro-001; TCA 2026-06-13T00:00:00Z; plan maneuver-plan-protect-isro-001; selected candidate mnv-protect-isro-a.

assumptions: Round 1 planner evaluates small impulsive along-track candidates only.
Miss-distance gain is approximated as delta-v times lead time times a documented fixture gain factor.
The selected maneuver is a simulation recommendation and still requires secondary screening.
Final-round work should replace the surrogate with post-burn orbit propagation and multi-axis optimization.
Post-burn miss-distance gain uses a documented Round 1 along-track lead-time surrogate.
Pc is recomputed with the same covariance model used for the pre-maneuver conjunction.
Round 1 secondary screening uses deterministic fixture results tied to the maneuver candidate.
Final-round screening should propagate the post-burn protected-object trajectory and re-run conjunction screening.
Estimated Pc is not operational-grade without real covariance.
The maneuver is a simulation recommendation, not spacecraft command authority.
